chapter: Chapter 5: The Fundamental Unit of Life

questions.question: Explain why mitochondria are called semi-autonomous organelles.
questions.answer: Mitochondria are called semi-autonomous organelles because they have their own DNA and ribosomes and can synthesize some of their own proteins.

questions.question: Explain plasmolysis with an example.
questions.answer: Plasmolysis is the shrinking of cytoplasm away from the cell wall when a plant cell loses water in a hypertonic solution. Example: onion peel in salt solution.

questions.question: Explain the structure and function of the plasma membrane.
questions.answer: The plasma membrane is a thin selectively permeable membrane that surrounds the cell and regulates entry and exit of substances.

questions.question: Describe the structure of the nucleus.
questions.answer: The nucleus consists of a nuclear membrane, nucleoplasm and chromosomes which carry genetic information.

questions.question: Explain the functions of lysosomes.
questions.answer: Lysosomes contain digestive enzymes that break down waste materials and damaged cell parts.

questions.question: Why is the plasma membrane called selectively permeable?
questions.answer: It is called selectively permeable because it allows only certain substances to pass through while restricting others.

questions.question: Explain the functions of the endoplasmic reticulum.
questions.answer: Endoplasmic reticulum forms channels for transport of materials and helps in synthesis of proteins and lipids.

questions.question: Differentiate between rough ER and smooth ER.
questions.answer: Rough ER has ribosomes and synthesizes proteins, whereas smooth ER lacks ribosomes and synthesizes lipids.

questions.question: Explain the role of Golgi apparatus in the cell.
questions.answer: Golgi apparatus modifies, packages and transports proteins and lipids to different parts of the cell.

questions.question: Explain why lysosomes are called suicide bags.
questions.answer: Lysosomes contain enzymes that can digest cell components and sometimes destroy the entire cell.

questions.question: Describe the structure of chromosomes.
questions.answer: Chromosomes are thread-like structures composed of DNA and proteins that carry genetic information.

questions.question: Explain the role of chloroplasts in plant cells.
questions.answer: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll which absorbs sunlight and carries out photosynthesis.

questions.question: Explain the function of vacuoles in plant cells.
questions.answer: Vacuoles store water, nutrients and wastes and help maintain turgidity of plant cells.

questions.question: Why are plant cells more rigid than animal cells?
questions.answer: Plant cells have a rigid cell wall made of cellulose which provides strength and rigidity.

questions.question: Explain the importance of diffusion in cells.
questions.answer: Diffusion allows gases like oxygen and carbon dioxide to move in and out of the cell.

questions.question: Explain the importance of osmosis in plants.
questions.answer: Osmosis helps plant roots absorb water from the soil.

questions.question: Differentiate between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.
questions.answer: Prokaryotic cells lack a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles, whereas eukaryotic cells have them.

questions.question: Explain why cells are called the basic unit of life.
questions.answer: Cells are the smallest units capable of performing all vital life processes.

questions.question: Describe the role of DNA in cells.
questions.answer: DNA stores hereditary information and controls the synthesis of proteins.

questions.question: Explain the importance of mitosis.
questions.answer: Mitosis produces identical daughter cells for growth and repair.

questions.question: Explain the importance of meiosis.
questions.answer: Meiosis produces gametes with half the chromosome number and maintains genetic stability.

questions.question: Explain how ribosomes help in protein synthesis.
questions.answer: Ribosomes read genetic instructions from RNA and assemble amino acids to form proteins.

questions.question: Explain the role of genes in heredity.
questions.answer: Genes are units of DNA that carry information responsible for inheritance of traits.

questions.question: Explain why plant cells have chloroplasts.
questions.answer: Plant cells have chloroplasts because they perform photosynthesis.

questions.question: Explain why cells divide.
questions.answer: Cells divide for growth, repair of damaged tissues and reproduction.
